description: Write and execute a PySAM Python script to evaluate the thermal derating penalty of a roof-mounted PV system compared to an open-rack ground mount. Use a TMY weather file (e.g., Phoenix) located in the directory path found in /home/ga/.SAM/solar_resource_dir.txt. Configure a PySAM.Pvwattsv8 model with 100.0 kW DC capacity, Standard module type, 20-degree tilt, 180-degree azimuth, 14.0% system losses, 1.2 DC/AC ratio, and 96.0% inverter efficiency. Compare an Open Rack configuration (array_type 0) and a Roof Mount configuration (array_type 1), recording annual energy and mean cell temperature for each. Calculate the energy penalty percentage and save the results to /home/ga/Documents/SAM_Projects/thermal_derating_comparison.json using the following structure: {"system_capacity_kw": 100.0, "weather_file_used": "<full_path_to_csv>", "open_rack": {"annual_energy_kwh": <float>, "mean_cell_temp_c": <float>}, "roof_mount": {"annual_energy_kwh": <float>, "mean_cell_temp_c": <float>}, "thermal_derating_penalty_percent": <float>}.
